inputs.prompt: Story: New research from a university urban planning department shows that cities implementing 15-minute neighbourhood policies saw a 22% reduction in car trips within 3 years — the largest study of its kind, covering 8 cities. Journalist: Lisa Chen, transport correspondent, The National Post. Recent article: She wrote about congestion pricing last month. Offer: Exclusive interview with the lead researcher before the study is published next week. Spokesperson: Prof. David Okafor, University of Westfield. My name: Tom Reid, Communications Manager, University of Westfield.

Story: A local bakery has been named in the top 50 bakeries in the country by a food magazine. Journalist: Food editor at the regional newspaper. Offer: Interview with the owner. My name: Anna Smith, bakery owner.

Story: Nordic Health Initiative has published the first cross-border study comparing cancer treatment outcomes across all five Nordic countries, revealing significant disparities despite similar healthcare systems. The study covers 200,000 patient records over 10 years. Journalist: Hans Eriksen, health correspondent, Politiken (Denmark). Recent article: He covered Nordic healthcare staffing shortages last quarter. Offer: Embargoed briefing with the study lead 48 hours before publication, plus access to the full dataset. Spokesperson: Dr. Ingrid Nørgaard, Executive Director. My name: Katrine Holm, Head of Communications, Nordic Health Initiative.

Story: A documentary filmmaker has spent three years embedded with families affected by the Grenfell Tower fire, resulting in a feature-length documentary premiering at a major film festival next month. Journalist: Claire Simmons, arts and culture editor, The Observer. No prior relationship. Recent article: She reviewed a documentary about housing inequality six months ago. Offer: Private screening link and interview with the filmmaker. Note: The filmmaker specifically does not want the pitch to lead with 'tragedy' framing — the film is about community resilience, not victimhood. My name: David Park, Publicist, Park Media.

Story: A Saudi Arabian edtech startup has raised $85 million Series B to expand Arabic-language AI tutoring across 14 countries. The product adapts to different Arabic dialects (Gulf, Levantine, Egyptian, Maghreb). Journalist: Nadia Al-Rashid, tech reporter, Al-Monitor. Recent article: She covered the Saudi Vision 2030 tech investment landscape last month. Offer: Exclusive interview with the female co-founder (notable in the Saudi context). Spokesperson: Reem Al-Harbi, Co-Founder & CEO. My name: Fahad Khalil, Head of PR, Abjad Education.

Story: The company is launching a significant new environmental programme — genuinely substantive, not greenwashing. Journalist: Mark Torres, environment correspondent, The Guardian. Problem: Torres published a highly critical article about the company's pollution record 8 months ago. The article was accurate. The pitch must acknowledge the previous coverage honestly without being defensive or sycophantic. Offer: Site visit to the new facility, access to independent audit results, and interview with the Chief Sustainability Officer. My name: Elena Voss, Director of Communications, Meridian Industries.
